title: manihot gracilis subsp. varians
intro: Manihot gracilis subsp. varians, also known as the yacón, is a perennial plant native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its edible tubers and sweet, molasses-like sap. The yacón plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of yacón consumption include improved digestion, reduced blood sugar levels, and potential weight loss, as well as its use as a natural sweetener. Yacón is often prepared in herbal tea form, as well as being used in its raw form, or made into a sweetener or syrup through the extraction of its sap.
